name: Schedule social posts with Google Sheets and WordPress with instant alerts

parameters.text: =✅ Schedule social posts with Google Sheets and WordPress with instant al — run completed at {{ $now.toFormat('HH:mm') }}

parameters.content:
### Weekly Schedule

Starts the workflow on a timer — weekly. No input data; downstream nodes fetch what they need.

Next: Read Content Calendar (Google Sheets)

### Read Content Calendar (Google Sheets)

Reads every row from 'Post Tracker' — the read side of the store, e.g. for a digest or a downstream sync.

Ports: ①→ Due for Publishing? · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Due for Publishing?

Drops items that fail the condition; only qualifying items continue (no false branch).

Next: Post One at a Time

### Post One at a Time

Loop controller — releases 1 item(s) per pass. Output 2 (loop) processes the batch, output 1 (done) fires after the last batch.

Ports: ①→ Count Published · ②→ Publish to Slack

nodes.name: Note: Post One at a Time

parameters.content:
### Publish to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#social`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

Ports: ①→ Space Out Posts · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Space Out Posts

Pauses 30 seconds — spacing calls to respect rate limits.

Next: Mark Row Published

### Mark Row Published

Upserts a row into 'Post Tracker', matched on `postId` — re-running with the same key updates the row instead of duplicating it.

Ports: ①→ Post One at a Time · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Count Published

Collapses all items into a single summary item (for counts and run stats).

Next: Stamp Completion Time

### Stamp Completion Time

Stamps `completedAt` with the current time for auditing.

Next: Persist Run Metadata

### Persist Run Metadata

Saves key fields onto the execution record — searchable later in n8n's execution list.

Next: Enrich with Reference Data

### Enrich with Reference Data

GET https://enrichment.example.com/v2/lookup — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Ports: ①→ Fold In Enrichment Fields · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Fold In Enrichment Fields

Shapes the item: writes `enrichedAt`, `enrichmentSource`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Load Previous Snapshot

### Load Previous Snapshot

Reads every row from 'Post Tracker' — the read side of the store, e.g. for a digest or a downstream sync.

Ports: ①→ Compute Drift vs Snapshot · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Compute Drift vs Snapshot

Custom JavaScript: Compare current metrics with the previous snapshot

Next: Fingerprint for Idempotency

### Fingerprint for Idempotency

Custom JavaScript: Build a stable idempotency key per post (djb2 hash, no deps)

Next: Drop Already-Processed Items

### Drop Already-Processed Items

Cross-execution dedupe: remembers keys it has processed before and drops repeats — the idempotency guard.

Next: Summarize Run Stats

### Summarize Run Stats

Shapes the item: writes `runAt`, `itemCount`, `status`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

### Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

Appends a new row to 'Post Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

Ports: ①→ Send Summary to Slack · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Send Summary to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#social`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Slack

### Escalate Failure to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#social`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

Next: Park in Dead-Letter Queue

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Park in Dead-Letter Queue

Appends a new row to 'Post Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

# Schedule social posts with Google Sheets and WordPress with instant alerts

**Pattern:** Social Scheduler · **Level:** Production · **Trigger:** Scheduled

## What it does
Schedule social posts with Google Sheets and WordPress with instant alerts. It runs automatically on a schedule.

## Why it exists
Manual listening is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Weekly Schedule → Read Content Calendar (Google Sheets) → Due for Publishing? → Post One at a Time → Count Published → Stamp Completion Time → Persist Run Metadata → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to social scheduler without manual effort
- Anyone automating listening in marketing
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Scales without extra headcount
- Has retries and error handling built in
- Responds in seconds

## Before first run
Connect credentials: API key / bearer for the target API; Google OAuth2 / service account; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

Posts go out one at a time through a batch-of-1 loop with a Wait between each, not all at once — most social APIs rate-limit bursts, and spacing also just reads as more natural than five posts landing in the same second. A row is marked published only after the post succeeds, so a mid-run crash resumes cleanly instead of re-posting everything.

## Practice exercises

Already production-grade — deepen your understanding:

1. Change the spacing delay and see how total run time scales with calendar size.
2. Add a second platform and post to both for each due item.
3. Manually mark one row as already-published and confirm the filter skips it.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Failures are also parked in a dead-letter queue for replay. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## ⏱ Rate limiting

Work is batched and spaced with Wait so provider quotas aren't tripped. If you hit 429s, lower the batch size or raise the wait — throughput drops, reliability rises.

## 🧹 Data quality

Dedupe/diff steps make re-runs safe: the same input twice will not create duplicate records. If you change the key fields, clear n8n's dedupe memory (node → 'Clear stored keys') before the next run.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your listening process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.

json.content: Behind the scenes: how we build workflow templates
